taskVersion.name: epu_q2_long
taskVersion.definition:
@kbench.task(name="epu_q2_long")
def epu_q2_long(
    llm,
    article_key: str,
    content_for_eval: str,
    gold_epu: int,
    short_pre: str = "",
    short_post: str = "",
    medium_pre: str = "",
    medium_post: str = "",
    long_pre: str = "",
    long_post: str = "",
    disagreement_flag: int = 0,
) -> float:
    out = model_json_call(llm, build_q2_prompt(content_for_eval, long_pre, long_post))
    m = binary_match(out["epu"], gold_epu)
    return 0.0 if m is None else float(m)

parts.text:
You are evaluating only the text between TARGET ARTICLE START and TARGET ARTICLE END.

Task:
Decide whether the TARGET ARTICLE expresses ECONOMIC POLICY UNCERTAINTY (EPU).

Labeling rule:
- EPU = 1 only if the TARGET ARTICLE discusses uncertainty about government policy,
  policy decisions, possible policy actions, political outcomes affecting policy,
  or the economic effects of policy.
- EPU = 0 for general economics, elections, markets, war, uncertainty, or foreign events
  unless the uncertainty is specifically tied to economic policy.

IMPORTANT:
- Use only the text inside TARGET ARTICLE START and TARGET ARTICLE END for the decision.
- Return ONLY valid JSON.
- No markdown. No explanation. No code fence.

Return exactly these keys:
{
  "epu": 0 or 1,
  "confidence": number between 0 and 1,
  "focus_excerpt": "short quote from target article only"
}

TARGET ARTICLE START
Center officials decided to seek the financing because interest rates are so low and are expected to rise in the next few months, [Jerry Mandel] said. Previously, Mandel had said the center would not consider a bond offering until it had raised $150 million. Mandel said center officials have been seeking gifts of at least $1 million. Most large pledges are paid over time and some donors have sought extensions, Mandel said, although almost no one has backed out. Concert hall funding; CREDIT: Los Angeles Times; OLD AND NEW: The Orange County Performing Arts Center's existing hall overltoks the new hall in progress. Fundraising has lagged since 1999, with the economy getting most of the blame. With interest rates still low, arts officials decided the time for bonds is now.; PHOTOGRAPHER: Mark Boster Los Angeles Times; HELP ON THE WAY: The basement and first fltor take shape in the new structure, just east of South Coast Plaza.; PHOTOGRAPHER: Mark Boster Los Angeles Times; SEATS APLENTY: Construction of the new performance hall, with the existing hall in the foreground, began in July. The new structure will have a 2,000-seat concert hall and 500-seat theater.; PHOTOGRAPHER: Mark Boster Los Angeles Times Center officials decided to seek the financing because interest rates are so low and are expected to rise in the next few months, [Jerry Mandel] said. Previously, Mandel had said the center would not consider a bond offering until it had raised $150 million. Mandel said center off
